get.description: Retrieve raw prompt template data without applying input variables. Designed for GitHub sync, local caching, and template inspection. By default, snippets are resolved (expanded). Use resolve_snippets=false to get the raw template with snippet references intact.

schema.title: identifier
schema.description: The identifier can be either the prompt name or the prompt id.

schema.title: Version
schema.description: Specific version number to retrieve. Mutually exclusive with `label`.

schema.title: Label
schema.description: Release label name to retrieve (e.g. 'prod', 'staging'). Mutually exclusive with `version`.

schema.title: Resolve Snippets
schema.description: When true (default), snippets are expanded in the returned prompt_template. When false, raw @@@snippet@@@ references are preserved.

schema.title: Include LLM Kwargs
schema.description: When true, includes provider-specific llm_kwargs in the response. Requires model metadata to be set on the template.

schema.title: identifier
schema.description: The identifier can be either the prompt name or the prompt id.

schema.title: identifier
schema.description: The identifier can be either the prompt name or the prompt id.

post.summary: Create a new label for a prompt

patch.description: Partially update a prompt template by creating a new version with merged changes. This endpoint fetches a base version (latest by default), applies your patches, validates the result, and creates a new version.

schema.title: Identifier
schema.description: The prompt template name or ID.

400.description: Validation error (e.g. conflicting fields, out-of-bounds index, wrong template type)

401.description: Unauthorized - missing or invalid API key

404.description: Prompt template, version, or label not found

request_id.description: The unique identifier for the request to which the metadata is associated.

metadata.description: A dictionary of metadata items to associate with the request. Can include session_ids, user_ids, location, etc.

request_id.description: The unique identifier for the request.

group_id.description: The unique identifier for the group to be associated with the request.

prompt_name.description: The name of the prompt template.

prompt_input_variables.description: Input variables for the prompt template.

request_id.description: The unique identifier for the request.

version.description: The version of the prompt template. Both version and label cannot be specified. Only one or none.

label.description: The label of the prompt template version. Both version and label cannot be specified. Only one or none.

score.description: The score you would like to give to this request (0 - 100).

name.description: A name for this request score. If not provided, the score will be tracked as `default`.

parameters.name: report_name
parameters.description: Name of the report to archive.

parameters.name: X-API-KEY
parameters.description: API key to authorize the operation.

post.description: Creates a new evaluation pipeline (report) with optional evaluation columns and custom scoring.

dataset_group_id.description: The ID of the dataset group containing the dataset versions to evaluate. The dataset group must be within a workspace accessible to the authenticated user.

name.description: Optional name for the evaluation pipeline. If not provided, a unique name will be auto-generated. Must be between 1 and 255 characters if specified.

folder_id.description: Optional folder ID to organize the pipeline within your workspace. If not specified, the pipeline will be created at the root level.

dataset_version_number.description: Optional specific dataset version number to use. If not specified, the latest non-draft version will be used. Cannot be -1 (draft version).

columns.description: Optional list of evaluation columns to create with the pipeline.

column_type.description: Type of column (e.g., LLM_ASSERTION, VARIABLE, CODE_EXECUTION, PROMPT_TEMPLATE)

name.description: Display name for the column

position.description: Position in the pipeline (auto-assigned if not provided)

is_part_of_score.description: Whether this column contributes to built-in score averaging (defaults to false)

code.description: Python or JavaScript code for score calculation. Receives 'data' variable (list of row dictionaries) and must return {'score': number}.

code_language.description: Language of the code (defaults to PYTHON)

success.description: Indicates if the operation was successful

report_id.description: The unique ID of the created evaluation pipeline. Use this ID to run evaluations.

report_columns.description: List of created columns (only present if columns were provided in the request)

external_ids.description: External ID mappings for the evaluation pipeline.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid parameters, draft dataset version, or no datasets in group

message.example: Dataset must have at least one non-draft version

403.description: Forbidden - Access to dataset group not allowed or missing permissions

message.example: Access to this dataset group is not allowed

404.description: Not Found - Dataset group or version not found

post.description:
Adds a new evaluation step (column) to an existing evaluation pipeline. Columns execute sequentially from left to right and can reference data from previous columns.

**Column Types Available:**
- **Primary**: PROMPT_TEMPLATE, ENDPOINT, MCP, HUMAN, CODE_EXECUTION, CODING_AGENT, CONVERSATION_SIMULATOR, WORKFLOW
- **Evaluation**: LLM_ASSERTION, AI_DATA_EXTRACTION, COMPARE, CONTAINS, REGEX, REGEX_EXTRACTION, COSINE_SIMILARITY, ABSOLUTE_NUMERIC_DISTANCE
- **Helper**: JSON_PATH, XML_PATH, PARSE_VALUE, APPLY_DIFF, VARIABLE, ASSERT_VALID, COALESCE, COMBINE_COLUMNS, COUNT, MATH_OPERATOR, MIN_MAX

See the full documentation for detailed configuration requirements for each column type.

report_id.description: The ID of the evaluation pipeline to add this column to.

column_type.description: The type of evaluation or transformation this column performs. Must be one of the supported column types.

name.description: Display name for this column. Must be unique within the pipeline. This name is used to reference the column in subsequent steps.

configuration.description: Column-specific configuration. The schema varies based on column_type. See documentation for each type's requirements.

position.description: Optional position for the column. If not specified, the column is added at the end. Cannot overwrite dataset columns.

report_column.description: Details of the created column including its ID and configuration

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid column type, configuration validation failed, or column name already exists

message.example: Report already has a column with that name

403.description: Forbidden - Cannot overwrite dataset columns or missing permissions

message.example: You can not overwrite dataset columns

404.description: Not Found - Report not found or not accessible

parameters.name: report_id
parameters.description: ID of the evaluation pipeline report to run.

parameters.name: X-API-KEY
parameters.description: API key to authorize the operation.

name.description: The name of the final report to be created. Must be between 1 and 255 characters in length.

dataset_id.description: The ID of the dataset to use for the report. If not provided, uses the evaluation pipeline's default dataset.

refresh_dataset.description: Whether to refresh the dataset before running the report. Only applicable for dynamic datasets.

report_id.description: The ID of the created final report.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid data or report generation error

401.description: Unauthorized - No requests found for the given criteria

403.description: Forbidden - Can only run evaluation pipeline reports or dataset refresh not allowed

404.description: Not Found - Report or dataset not found

patch.description: Rename or retag an evaluation pipeline. Provide name, tags, or both. Use this instead of recreating a misnamed pipeline.

404.description: Report not found or not accessible

patch.description: Update an existing column on an evaluation pipeline. Use this to fix a bug in a CODE_EXECUTION script, change a column's configuration, rename it, or reorder it — without recreating the whole pipeline. Cannot edit DATASET columns. Editing a column re-queues the affected cells.

parameters.name: report_column_id
parameters.description: ID of the report column to edit.

report_id.description: Parent evaluation pipeline ID. Must match the column's report.

column_type.description: Column type. DATASET is not allowed.

configuration.description: Replacement column configuration. Schema varies by column_type.

name.description: New column name. Must be unique within the pipeline.

position.description: New 1-based position. Cannot overwrite dataset columns.

400.description: Validation failed (invalid type/configuration, duplicate name, etc.)

403.description: Forbidden - Cannot edit DATASET columns or non-blueprint columns

404.description: Report column not found or not accessible

delete.description: Delete a single column from an evaluation pipeline. Surrounding columns shift left to fill the gap. Cannot delete DATASET columns. Cells in columns to the right of the deleted column are re-queued.

parameters.name: report_column_id
parameters.description: ID of the report column to delete.

404.description: Report column not found, or column is a DATASET column

get.description: Retrieve paginated rows from a dataset. Each row is an array of cells matching the order of the `columns` array. All cells have `{"type": "dataset", "value": ...}`.

post.summary: Create Dataset Version from Filter Params

parameters.name: X-API-KEY
parameters.description: API key for authentication. This endpoint supports API key authentication only.

schema.description: Send either `request_log_ids` (static snapshot) or `filter_group` + `q`/`sort_by`/`sort_order` (dynamic structured query). When both are present, `request_log_ids` wins.

dataset_group_id.description: ID of the dataset group where the new version will be created.

request_log_ids.description: Explicit list of request_log ids to include. Capped at 50,000. All ids must belong to the same workspace as the dataset group; cross-workspace ids return 400. Datasets created in this mode are static snapshots — `filter_params` is left null and the dataset cannot be refreshed via run-report.

filter_group.description: Structured filter group, identical in shape to the one accepted by `POST /api/public/v2/requests/search`. The full payload is persisted to the dataset so it can be replayed on refresh.

logic.description: How to combine the filters in this group.

value.description: Operator-specific value. Type depends on the operator (single value, list, or `[from, to]` range).

nested_key.description: For nested fields like `metadata`, the key inside the nested object to filter against.

q.description: Free-text search query applied alongside `filter_group`. Mirrors `q` from the unified request-log search.

sort_by.description: Field to sort the structured query by. Same allowed values as `POST /api/public/v2/requests/search` (e.g. `request_start_time`, `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `cost`, `latency_ms`, `status`).

sort_order.description: Sort direction. Defaults to `desc` when `sort_by` is provided.

variables_to_parse.description: List of input variables to extract as columns in the resulting dataset.

dataset_id.description: ID of the created draft dataset

version_number.description: Version number of the dataset (-1 for draft)

403.description: Access denied to this dataset group

draft_dataset_id.description: ID of the created draft dataset

202.description: Draft created, rows being copied from source dataset

draft_dataset_id.description: ID of the created draft dataset

403.description: Access denied to this dataset group

404.description: Dataset group or source dataset not found

409.description: A draft version already exists for this dataset group

get.description: Lists entities within a folder or at the workspace root. Returns folders, prompts, snippets, workflows, datasets, evaluations, AB tests, and input variable sets. Supports filtering by entity type, searching by name, flattening the folder hierarchy, and optionally including entity metadata.

schema.title: Workspace ID
schema.description: The ID of the workspace to list entities from.

schema.title: Folder ID
schema.description: The ID of the folder to list entities from. If not provided, lists entities at the workspace root level.

schema.title: Filter Type
schema.description: Filter entities by type. Can be a single type or a list of types. If not provided, all entity types are returned.

schema.title: Search Query
schema.description: Search entities by name (case-insensitive partial match).

schema.title: Flatten
schema.description: When true, returns all entities recursively within the folder hierarchy instead of only direct children.

schema.title: Include Metadata
schema.description: When true, includes type-specific metadata for each entity (e.g., prompt type, latest version number).

400.description: Bad request - Missing or invalid workspace_id

post.description: Moves one or more entities into a target folder, or to the workspace root if no folder_id is provided. Supports moving folders, prompts, snippets, workflows, datasets, evaluations, AB tests, and input variable sets. Requires appropriate edit permissions for each entity type.

400.description: Bad request - Missing workspace_id or invalid entity type

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid API key

delete.description: Deletes one or more entities from the workspace. Supports deleting folders, prompts, snippets, workflows, datasets, evaluations, AB tests, and input variable sets. Use cascade=true to recursively delete all contents of a folder. Without cascade, deleting a non-empty folder returns an error. Entities are soft-deleted where applicable.

400.description: Bad request - Folder is not empty (use cascade=true) or invalid entity type

401.description: Unauthorized - Missing or invalid API key

post.description:
Ingest OpenTelemetry traces using the standard OTLP/HTTP protocol.

This endpoint accepts an `ExportTraceServiceRequest` as defined by the [OpenTelemetry specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/protocol/otlp/#otlphttp). Spans carrying [GenAI semantic convention](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/) attributes are automatically converted into PromptLayer request logs.

Supported content types:
- `application/x-protobuf` — binary protobuf encoding (recommended)
- `application/json` — JSON encoding

Gzip `Content-Encoding` is supported for both formats.

Spans can include `promptlayer.prompt.name` (or `promptlayer.prompt.id`) and `promptlayer.prompt.version` (or `promptlayer.prompt.label`) attributes to link the generated request log to an existing prompt template in your workspace.

parameters.name: X-API-KEY
parameters.description: API key to authorize the operation.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: The encoding of the request body. Use `application/x-protobuf` for binary protobuf or `application/json` for JSON.

parameters.name: Content-Encoding
parameters.description: Set to `gzip` if the request body is gzip-compressed.

schema.description: Binary-encoded ExportTraceServiceRequest protobuf message.

200.description: Successful Response. When using protobuf, the response is a binary `ExportTraceServiceResponse`. When using JSON, the response is a JSON object.

schema.description: Binary-encoded ExportTraceServiceResponse protobuf message.

200.description: Paginated list of matching request logs.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded (10 requests per minute).

post.description: Aggregate analytics across request logs — totals, time-series breakdowns, latency percentiles, and model/prompt/provider/tag breakdowns. Body is the same `RequestLogQuery` shape as `POST /api/public/v2/requests/search` but returns aggregated values instead of paginated rows.

200.description: Aggregated analytics for the matching request logs.

400.description: Invalid filter or unsupported analytics filter.

403.description: Invalid workspace_id or plan limit reached.

CreateTableRequest.title: CreateTableRequest
CreateTableRequest.description: Create a Table with one default sheet, one text column, and one empty row.

title.description: Table title. If omitted, PromptLayer generates an Untitled Table name.

sheet_count.description: Number of active sheets in the table.
Number of active sheets in the table.

sheet_row_counts.description: Map of sheet ID to resolved row count.

row_count.description: Resolved row count for the sheet.

id.description: Cell UUID, or a virtual cell ID for not-yet-computed output cells.

value.description: Structured cell value. Shape depends on the column type.

TableImportOperation.title: TableImportOperation
TableImportOperation.description: Import operation fields returned by existing-sheet import endpoints.

file_content_base64.description: Base64-encoded CSV or JSON content. Maximum decoded file size is 100MB.

TableSheetRequestLogsSource.title: TableSheetRequestLogsSource
TableSheetRequestLogsSource.description: Import request logs into a new sheet. Provide either request_log_ids or filter_group.

request_log_ids.description: Specific request log IDs to import. All IDs must belong to the table workspace.

variables_to_parse.description: Input variable names to parse from matching request logs.

include_fields.description: Additional request log fields to include as columns.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching request logs to import.

title.description: New sheet title. Defaults to the imported file name or Request Logs.

index.description: Sheet index. Defaults to the next index in the table.

operation_id.description: Optional client-provided operation ID for polling and webhook correlation. A UUID is generated when omitted.

name.description: Name of the evaluation

comment.description: Optional comment or description for the evaluation

created_at.description: Timestamp when the evaluation was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the evaluation was last updated

workspace_id.description: ID of the workspace this evaluation belongs to

folder_id.description: ID of the folder containing this evaluation

user_id.description: ID of the user who created this evaluation

dataset_id.description: ID of the dataset associated with this evaluation

is_blueprint.description: Whether this is a blueprint (pipeline definition) or a batch run

deleted.description: Whether this evaluation has been deleted

parent_report_id.description: ID of the parent blueprint (set for batch runs)

score_configuration.description: Custom scoring configuration for this evaluation

runs.description: Batch runs for this evaluation. Only present when include_runs=true.

name.description: Name of the run

created_at.description: Timestamp when the run was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the run was last updated

user_id.description: ID of the user who created this run

deleted.description: Whether this run has been deleted

score_matrix.description: Matrix of scores across evaluation columns

score_calculation_error.description: Error message if score calculation failed

status.description: Current status of the batch run

snippet_name.description: The name of the snippet prompt template

prompt_registry_id.description: The ID of the prompt that uses this snippet

prompt_name.description: The name of the prompt that uses this snippet

version_numbers.description: List of version numbers that use this snippet

snippet_usage.description: List of prompts using this snippet

total_prompts_using_snippet.description: Total number of prompts using this snippet

prompt_registry_id.description: The ID of the prompt with a label using this snippet

prompt_name.description: The name of the prompt with a label using this snippet

label_name.description: The name of the label using this snippet

label_usage.description: List of labels using this snippet

total_labels_using_snippet.description: Total number of labels using this snippet

workflow_label_name.description: Specify a workflow label name to run a specific labeled version.

workflow_version_number.description: Specify a workflow version number to run a specific version.

metadata.description: A dictionary of metadata key-value pairs.

input_variables.description: A dictionary of input variables required by the workflow.

return_all_outputs.description: If set to `true`, all outputs from the workflow execution will be returned.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful.

warning.description: Any warnings about missing input variables.

workflow_version_execution_id.description: The ID of the created workflow execution.

RunWorkflowResponse.description: Response after initiating a workflow execution.

name.description: Unique name for the node within this workflow.

node_type.description: The type of node. Common types include VARIABLE, CODE_EXECUTION, PROMPT_TEMPLATE, ENDPOINT, COMPARE, CONTAINS, LLM_ASSERTION, AI_DATA_EXTRACTION, CODING_AGENT. See Node & Column Types documentation for the complete list.

dependencies.description: Names of nodes or input variables this node depends on.

is_output_node.description: Whether this node is an output node.

left_config.description: Left side of comparison. Can be static_value or source.

right_config.description: Right side of comparison. Can be static_value or source.

is_and.description: Whether multiple conditionals use AND logic (true) or OR logic (false).

name.description: The name for a new workflow. If not provided, a name will be auto-generated.

workflow_id.description: The ID of an existing workflow to create a new version for.

workflow_name.description: The name of an existing workflow to create a new version for.

folder_id.description: The folder ID to place the workflow in.

commit_message.description: A message describing the changes in this version.

required_input_variables.description: A mapping of variable names to their types.

release_labels.description: Labels to attach to this version.

CreateWorkflow.description: Request body for creating a new workflow or workflow version.

node_type.description: The node type (required for new nodes).

is_output_node.description: Whether this is an output node.

NodeUpdate.description: Partial update for a single node.

base_version.description: The version number to base changes on. Defaults to the latest version.

nodes.description: Node updates keyed by node name. Use null to remove a node.

required_input_variables.description: If provided, replaces the input variables entirely.

release_labels.description: Labels to attach to the new version.

PatchWorkflow.description: Request body for partially updating a workflow.

success.description: Indicates if the request was successful.

workflow_version_id.description: The ID of the created workflow version.

base_version.description: The base version this was created from (PATCH only).

nodes.description: Summary of nodes in the workflow.

required_input_variables.description: Required input variables for the workflow.

external_ids.description: External ID mappings for the workflow.

CreateWorkflowResponse.description: Response after creating or patching a workflow.

error.description: Error message explaining why the request failed.

SkillCollectionErrorResponse.description: Error response format returned by the public skill collection endpoints.

message.description: Error message explaining why the request failed.

SkillCollection.description: A skill collection container in the public API.

SkillCollectionVersion.description: A saved version of a skill collection.

external_ids.description: External ID mappings for the skill collection.

strict.title: Strict
strict.description: Whether to enable strict schema validation for the function parameters.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: The LLM provider (e.g. openai, anthropic, google, openai.azure, vertexai, mistral, cohere, amazon.bedrock, huggingface).

model_config_display_name.title: Model Config Display Name
model_config_display_name.description: Optional display name for the model configuration.

base_model.title: Base Model
base_model.description: The base model name (e.g. for fine-tuned models).

name.title: Name
name.description: The model name (e.g. gpt-4o, claude-sonnet-4-20250514).

display_params.title: Display Params
display_params.description: Display-friendly parameter values shown in the UI.

api_type.title: Api Type
api_type.description: The API type (e.g. chat.completions, responses, images). Used to select the correct API endpoint for the provider.

Metadata.title: Metadata
Metadata.description: Metadata associated with the prompt blueprint. Supports additional custom fields beyond the model field.

metadata_filters.title: Metadata Filters
metadata_filters.description: Optional dictionary of key values used for A/B release labels.

model.title: Modal
model.description: Optional model name used for returning default parameters with llm_kwargs.

model_parameter_overrides.title: Model Parameter Overrides
model_parameter_overrides.description: Optional dictionary of model parameter overrides to use with the prompt template. This will override the parameters at runtime for the specified model and will try to make sure the model supports these parameters. For example, if you supply `maxOutputTokens` for OpenAI, it will be converted to `max_completion_tokens`.

annotations.title: Annotations
annotations.description: Citations and references within the text (web citations, file citations, map citations, container file citations).

type.title: Type
type.description: MIME type of the media. For example, image/png, image/jpeg

name.title: Name
name.description: Name of the media variable

OutputMediaContent.description: LLM-generated media output (e.g. from image generation tools).
OutputMediaContent.title: OutputMediaContent

CodeContent.description: Code content block (e.g. from code execution tools).

ServerToolUseContent.description: Server-side tool use block (e.g. web search, code execution).

WebSearchToolResultContent.description: Results from a web search tool invocation.
WebSearchToolResultContent.title: WebSearchToolResultContent

CodeExecutionResultContent.description: Result from a code execution tool.

BashCodeExecutionToolResultContent.description: Result from bash code execution tool.

TextEditorCodeExecutionToolResultContent.description: Result from text editor code execution tool.
TextEditorCodeExecutionToolResultContent.title: TextEditorCodeExecutionToolResultContent

BuiltInTool.description: A provider-native built-in tool (e.g. web search, code interpreter, bash).

config.title: Config
config.description: Provider-specific tool configuration. Structure varies by provider and tool type.

function_call.title: Function Call
function_call.description: This field is deprecated. Please use `tool_calls` field to specify tool calls.

llm_kwargs.title: Llm Kwargs
llm_kwargs.description: When you optionally specify `provider` in the body, `llm_kwargs` will be returned for that specific provider and you can pass these kwargs to the provider's API directly. **Important:** This object's structure is provider-specific and may change without notice as LLM providers update their APIs. For stable, provider-agnostic prompt data, use `prompt_template` instead.

prompt_name.title: Prompt Name
prompt_name.description: The name of the snippet prompt template.

version.title: Version
version.description: The version number of the snippet used.

label.title: Label
label.description: The release label of the snippet, if applicable.

prompt_name.title: Prompt Name
prompt_name.description: The name of the prompt template.

version.title: Version
version.description: The version number of the prompt template.

workspace_id.title: Workspace Id
workspace_id.description: The workspace this prompt template belongs to.

prompt_template.title: Prompt Template
prompt_template.description: The prompt template content. When resolve_snippets is true (default), snippets are expanded. When false, raw @@@snippet@@@ references are preserved.

metadata.description: Model configuration including provider, model name, and parameters.

commit_message.title: Commit Message
commit_message.description: The commit message for this version.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags associated with the prompt template.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp when this version was created.

snippets.title: Snippets
snippets.description: List of snippet references used in this template.

llm_kwargs.title: LLM Kwargs
llm_kwargs.description: Provider-specific LLM arguments. Only present when include_llm_kwargs=true. Structure is provider-specific and may change without notice.

prompt_name.title: Prompt Name
prompt_name.description: The unique prompt name within the workspace. 1-512 characters.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: Tags to attach to the prompt registry entry.

folder_id.title: Folder Id
folder_id.description: The ID of the folder to publish the prompt template into. If omitted, the prompt is created at the workspace root. Use Resolve Folder ID by Path to look up an ID from a path, or Create Folder to make one.

workspace_id.title: Workspace Id
workspace_id.description: Optional workspace override; defaults to the workspace associated with the API key.

prompt_template.title: Prompt Template
prompt_template.description: The prompt content, either chat or completion.

commit_message.title: Commit Message
commit_message.description: Message describing the changes in this version. Maximum 72 characters.

metadata.description: Metadata to attach to the prompt version.

version.title: Version
version.description: The base version number to patch from. Mutually exclusive with `label`. If neither is provided, the latest version is used.

label.title: Label
label.description: The release label identifying the base version to patch from (e.g. 'prod', 'staging'). Mutually exclusive with `version`.

anyOf.description: Index-based patch. Keys are string indices (e.g. "0", "1"), values are the replacement message objects.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: Patch for chat template messages. Pass an object with index keys for index-based patching, or an array for full replacement. Chat templates only.

tools.title: Tools
tools.description: Patch for tools. Object for index-based patching, array for full replacement, null to remove. Chat templates only.

functions.title: Functions
functions.description: Patch for functions. Object for index-based patching, array for full replacement, null to remove. Chat templates only.

function_call.title: Function Call
function_call.description: Replace the function_call setting. Set to null to remove. Chat templates only.

tool_choice.title: Tool Choice
tool_choice.description: Replace the tool_choice setting. Set to null to remove. Chat templates only.

content.title: Content
content.description: Patch for completion template content. Object for index-based patching, array for full replacement. Completion templates only.

model_parameters.title: Model Parameters
model_parameters.description: Parameters to shallow-merge into the existing model parameters (e.g. temperature, max_tokens). Existing keys not specified here are preserved.

response_format.title: Response Format
response_format.description: Convenience field to set response_format in model parameters. Cannot be used simultaneously with response_format inside model_parameters. Set to null to remove.

commit_message.title: Commit Message
commit_message.description: A message describing the changes in this version.

release_labels.title: Release Labels
release_labels.description: Release labels to create or move to the newly created version (e.g. ['staging', 'production']).

prompt_template.description: Template metadata, including prompt name, tags, folder, and workspace fields.

release_labels.title: Release Labels
release_labels.description: Release labels to create or move to the newly created version.

id.title: Id
id.description: The ID of the prompt template.

prompt_name.title: Prompt Name
prompt_name.description: The name of the prompt template.

prompt_version_id.title: Prompt Version Id
prompt_version_id.description: The ID of the created prompt version.

version_number.title: Version Number
version_number.description: The version number of the prompt template.

external_ids.title: External Ids
external_ids.description: External ID mappings attached to the prompt template.

parameters.title: Parameters
parameters.description: Model parameters including temperature, max_tokens, etc. Can also include structured output configuration via response_format.json_schema. See documentation for structured output examples.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Custom key-value pairs for tracking additional request information. Keys are limited to 1024 characters.

prompt_id.title: Prompt Id
prompt_id.description: The ID of the prompt template used for this request. This is useful for tracking which prompt was used in the request.

status.title: Status
status.description:
Request status.

| Value | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `SUCCESS` | Request completed successfully (default) |
| `WARNING` | Request succeeded but had issues (e.g., retries, degraded response) |
| `ERROR` | Request failed |

error_type.title: Error Type
error_type.description:
Categorized error type.

| Value | Description | Allowed Statuses |
|-------|-------------|------------------|
| `PROVIDER_RATE_LIMIT` | Rate limit hit on provider API | WARNING, ERROR |
| `PROVIDER_QUOTA_LIMIT` | Account quota or spending limit exceeded | WARNING, ERROR |
| `PROVIDER_PARTIAL_RESPONSE` | Provider returned a successful response, but the saved output may be incomplete, filtered, blocked, malformed, or otherwise partial | WARNING |
| `VARIABLE_MISSING_OR_EMPTY` | Required template variable was missing or empty | WARNING |
| `PROVIDER_TIMEOUT` | Request timed out | ERROR |
| `PROVIDER_AUTH_ERROR` | Authentication failed with provider | ERROR |
| `PROVIDER_ERROR` | General provider-side error | ERROR |
| `TEMPLATE_RENDER_ERROR` | Failed to render prompt template | ERROR |
| `UNKNOWN_ERROR` | Uncategorized error | WARNING, ERROR |

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: Detailed error message describing what went wrong. Maximum 1024 characters.

status.title: Status
status.description: Request status indicating success, warning, or error.

error_type.title: Error Type
error_type.description: Categorized error type if status is WARNING or ERROR.

error_message.title: Error Message
error_message.description: Detailed error message if status is WARNING or ERROR.

prompt_template.title: Prompt Template
prompt_template.description: The prompt template. Either a CompletionPrompt (type: 'completion') or ChatPrompt (type: 'chat').

provider_base_url_name.title: Provider Base Url Name
provider_base_url_name.description: Name of the custom provider base URL configuration.

inference_client_name.title: Inference Client Name
inference_client_name.description: Name of the inference client to use.

provider_id.title: Provider Id
provider_id.description: ID of the provider configuration to use.

column_names.description: Array of column names in the dataset

filter_params.description: Filter parameters used to create the dataset

user_id.description: ID of the user who created the dataset

name.description: Dataset group name

is_deleted.description: Whether the dataset group is deleted

name.description: The name of the folder. Must be unique within its parent folder.

parent_id.description: The ID of the parent folder. If null or not provided, the folder will be created at the root level of the workspace.

workspace_id.description: Optional workspace ID. If not provided, uses the workspace associated with your API key.

success.description: Indicates if the operation was successful

name.description: The name of the folder

created_at.description: Timestamp when the folder was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the folder was last updated

path.description: JSON array representing the folder hierarchy path. Contains the IDs and names of all parent folders. Null for root-level folders.

workspace_id.description: ID of the workspace this folder belongs to

parent_id.description: ID of the parent folder. Null for root-level folders.

external_ids.description: External ID mappings for the folder.

name.description: The new name for the folder. Must be unique within its parent folder.

EntityType.description: The type of entity. SNIPPET is a pseudo-entity type representing completion-type prompts.

name.description: Name of the entity

created_at.description: Timestamp when the entity was created

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the entity was last updated

folder_id.description: ID of the folder containing this entity. Null for root-level entities.

path.description: Array of folder names representing the entity's location in the folder hierarchy.

metadata.description: Type-specific metadata. Included only when include_metadata=true.

latest_version_number.description: The latest version number of the prompt.
The latest version number of the workflow.

isDraft.description: Whether the dataset group only has draft versions (no published versions).

latest_version_number.description: The latest published version number of the dataset.

entities.description: List of entities matching the query.

folder_id.description: The ID of the destination folder. If null or not provided, entities are moved to the workspace root.

cascade.description: When true, recursively deletes all contents of any folders in the entities list. When false, attempting to delete a non-empty folder returns an error.

moved_count.description: The number of entities affected by the operation.

id.description: The ID of the resolved folder.

OtlpExportTraceServiceRequest.title: ExportTraceServiceRequest
OtlpExportTraceServiceRequest.description: An OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest in JSON encoding. See the [OTLP specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otlp/#otlphttp-request) for the full schema.

resourceSpans.description: An array of ResourceSpans. Each element describes spans from a single instrumented resource.

resource.description: The resource that produced the spans.

scopeSpans.description: An array of ScopeSpans, grouping spans by instrumentation scope.

scope.description: The instrumentation scope that produced the spans.

OtlpSpan.title: Span
OtlpSpan.description: A single OTLP span. See the [OTLP Span spec](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/trace/api/#span) for full details.

traceId.description: Hex-encoded trace ID (32 hex characters / 16 bytes).

spanId.description: Hex-encoded span ID (16 hex characters / 8 bytes).

parentSpanId.description: Hex-encoded parent span ID. Empty or omitted for root spans.

name.description: The name of the span.

startTimeUnixNano.description: Start time in nanoseconds since Unix epoch, encoded as a string.

endTimeUnixNano.description: End time in nanoseconds since Unix epoch, encoded as a string.

attributes.description: Span attributes as key-value pairs. GenAI semantic convention attributes (e.g. `gen_ai.request.model`, `gen_ai.usage.input_tokens`) are automatically extracted.

message.description: Status message (typically set for ERROR status).

value.description: The attribute value. Exactly one of the value fields must be set.

OtlpExportTraceServiceResponse.title: ExportTraceServiceResponse
OtlpExportTraceServiceResponse.description: Response to an OTLP trace export request.

partialSuccess.description: Present only when some spans were rejected. Null when all spans were accepted.

rejectedSpans.description: The number of spans that were rejected.

errorMessage.description: A human-readable error message describing why spans were rejected.

RequestLogQuery.title: RequestLogQuery
RequestLogQuery.description: Canonical request-log query payload — the filter / search / sort fields shared by `POST /api/public/v2/requests/search` (which also accepts pagination + `include_prompt_name`) and `POST /api/public/v2/requests/analytics`.

filter_group.description: Nested filter group with AND/OR logic. Use this for complex queries.

q.description: Free-text search query. Searches across the prompt input and LLM output text using fuzzy prefix matching.

sort_by.description: Field to sort results by. Does not affect aggregated output for `/requests/analytics`.

sort_order.description: Sort direction. Must be provided together with sort_by.

RequestAnalyticsLatencyByDay.description: Per-bucket latency percentiles in seconds. Keys are bucket dates (e.g. `2025-03-15`); values are seconds.

RequestAnalyticsDimensionLatency.description: Per-dimension (model / prompt template / provider) latency series. Each percentile is a list of `[date, seconds]` pairs.

traceShare.description: Fraction of requests in the bucket that have a trace.

RequestAnalyticsResponse.title: RequestAnalyticsResponse
RequestAnalyticsResponse.description: Aggregated analytics across the matching request logs. Bucket size is selected automatically based on the filter time range (seconds → minutes → hours → days).

chartInterval.description: Bucket-interval metadata describing how the time-series was bucketed.

averageLatency.description: Overall average latency across all matching requests, in seconds.

cacheTokenRatio.description: `totalCachedTokens / total_input_tokens`, or null when there are no input tokens.

mostUsedModels.description: List of `[modelName, requestCount]` pairs ordered by usage.

modelRequestsByDay.description: Map of model name → list of `[date, requestCount]` pairs.

SearchRequestLogsRequest.title: SearchRequestLogsRequest
SearchRequestLogsRequest.description: Search and filter request logs with structured filters, free-text search, sorting, and pagination. Extends `RequestLogQuery` with pagination and `include_prompt_name`.

page.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1.

per_page.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 10, maximum 25.

include_prompt_name.description: When true, includes the prompt template name in each result item. Defaults to false.

StructuredFilter.title: StructuredFilter
StructuredFilter.description: A single filter condition on a request log field.

field.description: The request log field to filter on.

value.description: The value to compare against. Type depends on the field and operator.

nested_key.description: Required for nested fields (metadata, output, input_variables). Specifies which key within the nested object to filter on.

StructuredFilterGroup.title: StructuredFilterGroup
StructuredFilterGroup.description: A group of filters combined with AND or OR logic. Can be nested recursively.

logic.description: How to combine the filters in this group.

filters.description: List of filters or nested filter groups.

items.description: List of matching request log summaries.
A request log summary including metadata, token counts, cost, and tags.

has_next.description: Whether there are more pages after the current one.

has_prev.description: Whether there are pages before the current one.

next_num.description: Next page number, or null if on the last page.

prev_num.description: Previous page number, or null if on the first page.

source.title: Source
source.description: The external system or namespace that owns the ID.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: The identifier for this entity in the external system.

ExternalId.title: ExternalId
ExternalId.description: Customer-defined mapping between a PromptLayer entity and an external system identifier.

source.title: Source
source.description: The external system or namespace that owns the ID.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: The identifier for this entity in the external system.

AttachExternalIdRequest.title: AttachExternalIdRequest
AttachExternalIdRequest.description: External ID mapping to attach to an entity.

llm_kwargs.title: Llm Kwargs
llm_kwargs.description: When you optionally specify `provider` in the body, `llm_kwargs` will be returned for that specific provider and you can pass these kwargs to the provider's API directly. **Important:** This object's structure is provider-specific and may change without notice as LLM providers update their APIs. For stable, provider-agnostic prompt data, use `prompt_template` instead.

external_ids.title: External Ids
external_ids.description: External ID mappings attached to the prompt template.

UpdateTableRequest.title: UpdateTableRequest
UpdateTableRequest.description: Update a Table title or folder.

title.description: New Table title. Blank titles are rejected.

folder_id.description: Folder ID. Pass null to remove the Table from its folder.

UpdateTableSheetRequest.title: UpdateTableSheetRequest
UpdateTableSheetRequest.description: Update a Table sheet title or index.

title.description: New sheet title. Blank titles are rejected.

AddTableSheetRowsRequest.title: AddTableSheetRowsRequest
AddTableSheetRowsRequest.description: Append rows to a Table sheet. Values are keyed by column ID.

values.description: Optional per-row values keyed by column ID. Values beyond count are ignored; missing values use the column default behavior.

title.description: Column title. Blank titles are rejected.

config.description: Column configuration. Shape depends on column type.

title.description: New column title. Blank titles are rejected.

config.description: Updated column configuration. Shape depends on column type.

filters.description: Filters applied to this page. The cursor includes a hash of this filter set, so changing filters while reusing a cursor returns an invalid cursor error.

count.description: Number of items returned on this page.

requires_recalculation.description: Whether the config or dependencies changed and affected cells should be recalculated.

affected_column_ids.description: Column IDs affected by the config or dependency change. Empty when recalculation is not required.

display_value.description: Display value for a text-column cell.

value.description: Structured value for a text-column cell.

column_ids.description: Columns to recalculate. Text columns cannot be recalculated.

row_ids.description: Zero-based row indices to recalculate. If omitted, matching cells across all rows are considered.

statuses.description: Cell statuses to include. Defaults to stale cells. Pass an empty array to include all statuses.

confirmation_token.description: Confirmation token returned when the operation exceeds the confirmation threshold.

score_type.description: Scoring mode. Required when score_config is provided.

column_names.description: Column titles to score. Titles must be unique in the sheet.

true_values.description: Values treated as true for boolean scoring.

false_values.description: Values treated as false for boolean scoring.

assertion_aggregation.description: Aggregation mode for assertion-style boolean scoring.

status.description: Returned as null by this endpoint. Use the recalculation endpoint to queue score calculation.

requires_recalculation.description: Always true after updating the score configuration.

filters.description: Filters applied to this page. The cursor includes a hash of this filter set, so changing filters while reusing a cursor returns an invalid cursor error.

count.description: Number of items returned on this page.

name.description: Version name. Required when source_version_id is omitted.

source_version_id.description: Existing version to restore from while creating a new version.

operation_id.description: Optional client-provided operation ID. A UUID is generated when omitted.

TableSheetRequestLogImportRequest.title: TableSheetRequestLogImportRequest
TableSheetRequestLogImportRequest.description: Import request history into an existing Table sheet. Provide either request_log_ids or filter_group.

operation_id.description: Optional client-provided operation ID. A UUID is generated when omitted.

request_log_ids.description: Specific request log IDs to import.

variables_to_parse.description: Input variable names to parse from matching request logs.

include_fields.description: Additional request log fields to include as columns.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching request logs to import.

operation_id.description: Optional client-provided operation ID. A UUID is generated when omitted.

file_name.description: CSV file name. Must end with .csv.

CreateTableSheetRequestLogImportRequest.title: CreateTableSheetRequestLogImportRequest
CreateTableSheetRequestLogImportRequest.description: Import request history into an existing Table sheet. Provide either request_log_ids or filter_group.

operation_id.description: Optional client-provided operation ID. A UUID is generated when omitted.

request_log_ids.description: Specific request log IDs to import.

variables_to_parse.description: Input variable names to parse from matching request logs.

include_fields.description: Additional request log fields to include as columns.

limit.description: Maximum number of matching request logs to import.

PublicListPagination.title: PublicListPagination

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as cursor for the next page, or null when there is no next page. The cursor is valid only with the same sort, order, and filter parameters used to produce it.

prev_cursor.description: Reserved for previous-page cursors. Public table list endpoints currently return null.

PublicListPagination.description: Cursor pagination metadata for public table list endpoints. Cursors are opaque and include the active sort, order, cursor value, and a hash of the active filter set.

filters.description: Filters applied to this page. The cursor includes a hash of this filter set, so changing filters while reusing a cursor returns an invalid cursor error.

count.description: Number of items returned on this page.

filters.description: Filters applied to this page. The cursor includes a hash of this filter set, so changing filters while reusing a cursor returns an invalid cursor error.

count.description: Number of items returned on this page.

filters.description: Filters applied to this page. The cursor includes a hash of this filter set, so changing filters while reusing a cursor returns an invalid cursor error.

count.description: Number of items returned on this page.

columns.description: Column definitions when included; otherwise an empty array.

row_count.description: Resolved row count. Present unless include_row_count=false.

folder_id.description: Folder filter applied to the request.

name.description: Name filter applied to the request.

prompt_id.description: Prompt template filter applied to the request.

prompt_version_id.description: Prompt version filter applied to the request.

prompt_label_id.description: Prompt label filter applied to the request.

prompt_id.description: Prompt template filter applied to the request.

prompt_version_id.description: Prompt version filter applied to the request.

prompt_label_id.description: Prompt label filter applied to the request.

EmptyPublicFilters.title: EmptyPublicFilters
EmptyPublicFilters.description: This endpoint does not echo any filter fields.

row_count.description: Resolved row count for the sheet.

TableImportOperationSummary.title: TableImportOperationSummary
TableImportOperationSummary.description: Import operation fields returned by the table-level sheet import endpoints.
